description:
A conceptual design team has updated the sizing matrix for a regional jet concept. The required lifting surface areas have increased, but the aerodynamicists dictate that the Aspect Ratios must remain strictly identical to the baseline to preserve the spanwise lift distribution. Open the baseline model at `/home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/conceptual_jet_baseline.vsp3`. Resize the Main Wing (the largest wing) to exactly 420.0 m^2 and the Horizontal Tail (the smaller wing) to exactly 105.0 m^2. 

CRITICAL WORKFLOW INSTRUCTION: You must parametrically resize the wings using the 'Plan' tab. Set the driving parameters (the dropdowns next to the input boxes) to 'Area' and 'AR' (Aspect Ratio), then input the new Area. Do NOT use the 'XForm' Scale tab, as global scaling does not update the parametric planform variables correctly and will fail verification. The Fuselage geometry must remain completely unchanged. Save the updated model to `/home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/conceptual_jet_resized.vsp3`.
